name: ClassroomIO
version: Release state UNVERIFIED (secondary trackers suggest v0.3.2)
license: Review current repo license before bundling
what: Open-source classroom/LMS-style platform for courses, classes, and teaching workflows.
solves: Hosted classrooms/courses if Hylono wants structured educational programming.
users: Educators and teams building teaching workflows.
fit: Interesting only if Hylono plans a structured course/classroom product. Otherwise unnecessary and less mature than surrounding options.
runtime: Node monorepo with API/dashboard/docs and local infra dependencies such as Docker and Supabase CLI.
components: API, dashboard, docs, and supporting local infrastructure/dev tooling.
deploy: Self-hosting appears possible, but maturity and release/version clarity are weak compared with other tools in this stack.
deps: Node 22+, Docker, Supabase-related tooling, and further architecture validation.
datastores: Likely relational/backend services via Supabase stack; verify before implementation.
auth: Likely app-level auth through the platform; verify current state.
apis: Repo components suggest API/dashboard split, but production surfaces need validation.
security: Course/community user data and classroom interactions require standard controls.
setup_path: Do not prioritize without a firm educational-product requirement and fresh verification.
env: Node, Docker, Supabase/local env config, app secrets, and deployment-specific settings.
persistence: Depends on backend choice; verify architecture first.
operations: Project maturity and deployment support are the main concerns.
failure_points: Implementing a less mature classroom product when simpler content/community/video tools may suffice.
integration: Could matter only if Hylono launches formal course/classroom offerings.
verify: Primary unresolved items: current release state, license, production readiness, and actual product need.
gdpr: User/classroom data would require standard handling.
recommendation: VERIFY FRESHNESS BEFORE ANY USE / LIKELY LATER
overlap: Could overlap with Jitsi + BookStack + Discourse/Docusaurus + payment/booking flows for simpler education use cases.
codex_read_first: Read the maturity and overlap notes first.
codex_best_path: Do not implement unless Hylono commits to a real classroom product and the project is revalidated.
codex_never_assume: Never trust stale or secondary version data for ClassroomIO.
